name: Recursive Leakage via Context Saturation
vector: Memory summarization leaks secrets into later completions

name: Heuristic Drift Injection
vector: Insecure patterns repeated over time bias agent behavior

name: Tool Confusion Attack
vector: Tool name similarity used to invoke wrong function

name: Token Gaslighting
vector: Spam tokens to push important context out of window

name: Function Flooding
vector: Agents generate too many tool calls via recursive planning

name: Subprompt Extraction
vector: Agent tricked into revealing config/system prompt

name: Hidden File Exploitation
vector: Agent edits .env, .git, or internal config files

name: Backdoor Planning
vector: Agent embeds hidden tasks in future plan steps

name: Nested Function Call Hijack
vector: Agent interprets user-supplied JSON as tool call

name: Semantic DoS
vector: Open-ended prompt causes infinite code/docs generation

name: Full-Schema Poisoning (FSP)
vector: Exploit any field in tool schema beyond descriptions

name: Advanced Tool Poisoning Attack (ATPA)
vector: Manipulate tool outputs to trigger secondary malicious actions

name: MCP Rug Pull Attack
vector: Serve benign descriptions during review, swap for malicious in production

name: Schema Validation Bypass
vector: Exploit differences in client validation implementations

name: Cross-Embedding Poisoning
vector: Manipulate vector embeddings to pull legitimate content closer to malicious embeddings

name: Index Skew Attacks
vector: Manipulate vector indices to bias nearest neighbor retrievals toward malicious content

name: Context Packing Attacks
vector: Inflate retrieved content to cause context window overflows and truncate safety instructions
defense: Content size limits in vector stores

name: Zero-Shot Vector Beaconing
vector: Embed latent activation patterns in vectors that signal specific behaviors without explicit instructions

name: Embedding Feedback Loops
vector: Poison embeddings re-ingested for continual learning to bias future outputs

name: EchoLeak (LLM Scope Violation)
vector: Zero-click indirect prompt injection exploiting LLM scope boundaries to exfiltrate data without user interaction
targets: Enterprise AI assistants with broad data access
defense: Input sanitization at document ingestion layer
Monitor outbound data requests during summarization

name: MemoryGraft (Poisoned Experience Retrieval)
vector: Persistent indirect injection targeting agent experience-based memory via crafted past task outcomes
defense: Anomaly detection on retrieved memory patterns

name: MCP Sampling Exploitation
vector: Exploit MCP sampling feature for covert tool invocation, conversation hijacking, and resource theft
targets: Claude and GPT agents with MCP integrations
defense: Monitor unexpected tool calls during sampling

name: Gemini Trifecta (Three-Vector Indirect Prompt Injection)
vector: Three coordinated vulnerabilities exploiting Google Gemini surfaces via indirect prompt injection
defense: Validate log inputs before LLM processing

name: CometJacking (Agentic Browser Hijack)
vector: URL-based prompt injection targeting AI browsers to hijack connected services
attribution: LayerX Security and Brave Security Research, August 2025
targets: Agentic browsers with connected service access
defense: Prohibit AI execution of instructions from URL query strings
Restrict data encoding during navigation events

name: Tainted Memories (CSRF-Based Persistent Memory Injection)
vector: Cross-Site Request Forgery exploiting AI browser auth to inject persistent malicious instructions into LLM memory
targets: LLMs with persistent memory and web browsing
defense: CSRF protections on memory write endpoints

name: Multi-Agent Control-Flow Hijacking (MAS-CFH)
vector: Fake error messages hijack orchestrator re-planning to invoke unsafe agents or arbitrary code execution

name: Inter-Agent Trust Exploitation
vector: Malicious payloads laundered through peer agents bypass direct injection defenses via blind inter-agent trust
attribution: arXiv:2507.06850 (The Dark Side of LLMs), July 2025
targets: All multi-agent systems with inter-agent communication
defense: Treat peer agent outputs as untrusted input

name: Delayed Tool Invocation (Asynchronous Memory Poisoning)
vector: Conditional deferred injection triggered by natural user responses in later conversation turns
targets: LLMs with persistent memory and tool use
defense: Separate authorization flows for memory writes
Do not allow ambient responses to authorize tool invocations

name: Slopsquatting (AI Hallucination Package Squatting)
vector: Register package names that AI coding agents hallucinate, turning fabricated dependencies into supply chain attacks
attribution: UT San Antonio, Virginia Tech, University of Oklahoma (USENIX Security 2025)
targets: AI coding agents (Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor)
defense: Hallucination detection in code generation output

name: Marketplace Skill Poisoning (OpenClaw / ClawHavoc)
vector: Unvetted public skill registries exploited for credential theft, RCE, and malware distribution
attribution: SecurityScorecard, Sangfor; CVE-2026-25253 et al., January-February 2026
targets: Agent ecosystems with unvetted plugin/skill marketplaces
defense: Mandatory code review for marketplace submissions

name: Semantic Privilege Escalation
vector: Privilege escalation at the semantic layer — agent takes unauthorized actions while passing every access control check
targets: Enterprise AI agents with broad tool access

name: MCP Supply Chain Backdoor (Postmark BCC Exfiltration)
vector: Compromised npm package in MCP email automation silently BCCs every outgoing email to attacker
defense: Monitor outbound email headers for unexpected BCC

name: Reasoning-Assisted Sandbox Escape
vector: AI agent autonomously discovers and chains sandbox bypass techniques through reasoning about its constraints

name: A2A Protocol Exploitation (Agent Card Spoofing / Cascading Delegation)
vector: Multiple attack surfaces in Google A2A protocol enabling impersonation, token abuse, and cascading agent compromise
attribution: Cloud Security Alliance, Semgrep, Solo.io (arXiv:2505.12490), April-December 2025
defense: Consent mechanisms for cross-agent data sharing
Treat all inter-agent input as untrusted

name: Phantom (Structural Template Injection)
vector: Exploit chat template tokens (system/user/assistant delimiters) to create fabricated conversation history inducing role confusion
targets: LLM agents using structured chat templates
defense: Strip role tokens in retrieved content

name: STAC (Sequential Tool-chain Attack via Composition)
vector: Chain individually benign tool calls into dangerous sequences; first automated multi-turn agentic jailbreak framework

name: Visual Prompt Injection (Computer-Use Agents)
vector: Visually embedded instructions in rendered UIs hijack computer-use agents processing screenshots
defense: Separate UI text from instructional text

name: CrossInject (Cross-Modal Prompt Injection)
vector: Adversarial perturbations across vision and language channels simultaneously hijack multimodal agent decision-making

name: Zombie Agents (Self-Reinforcing Persistent Injection)
vector: Payload exploits long-term memory update mechanisms, survives across sessions via self-reinforcing re-writes into memory
targets: Self-evolving LLM agents with persistent memory

name: MINJA (Memory Injection via Query-Only Interaction)
vector: Inject malicious records into agent memory bank solely through query interactions with 98.2% injection success rate

name: ToolHijacker (Tool Selection Manipulation)
vector: Inject malicious tool document into tool library to compel agents to choose attacker-controlled tools (96.43% ASR)
targets: LLM agents with retrieval-based tool selection

name: UDora (Reasoning Trace Hijacking)
vector: Identify optimal injection points within agent's own reasoning trace to insert targeted perturbations inducing malicious actions

name: Chain-of-Thought Hijacking
vector: Pad harmful requests with long harmless puzzle reasoning sequences to prime reasoning models into continuing into harmful content
targets: Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT o4 mini, Grok 3 mini, Claude 4 Sonnet

name: Email Agent Hijacking
vector: Override email agent system prompts via malicious email content for remote control without user awareness (1,404/1,404 instances hijacked)

name: DECEPTICON (Dark Pattern Manipulation of Web Agents)
vector: Deceptive UI designs manipulate web agents more effectively than humans; larger models are MORE susceptible
attribution: Phil Cuvin, Hao Zhu, Diyi Yang (arXiv:2512.22894), December 2025

name: Promptware Kill Chain
vector: Seven-stage kill chain for prompt-injection-based malware: 21 real-world incidents in 2025-2026, 15 demonstrated 4+ stages

name: Rule File Injection (Your AI, My Shell)
vector: Inject prompts into AI coding editor config files (.cursorrules, copilot-instructions.md) to hijack terminal execution
defense: User approval for rule file changes

name: CorruptRAG (Single-Document RAG Poisoning)
vector: High ASR from injecting only a single poisoned text into RAG knowledge base — no triggers or multiple documents needed

name: Prompt Infection (LLM-to-LLM Self-Replicating Worm)
vector: Self-replicating prompt injection propagates across agents in multi-agent systems like a computer virus (80%+ success on GPT-4o)

name: Parallel Poisoned Web (Agent-Specific Cloaking)
vector: Parallel website versions serve different content to AI agents vs. humans via fingerprinting — agents cannot detect the difference
targets: Any AI agent that browses the web

name: AgentFlayer (Zero-Click Enterprise Agent Exploit)
vector: Zero-click exploit chains via hidden instructions in documents (white 1-pixel font) — only requires victim's email address

name: Policy Puppetry (Universal Config-File Jailbreak)
vector: Adversarial prompts disguised as structured config/policy files universally bypass safety alignment across all frontier models
targets: All major frontier LLMs (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Llama, Mistral, Qwen)
defense: No complete fix — systemic LLM training weakness

name: LangGrinch (Serialization Injection)
vector: LLM-influenced metadata deserialized as executable objects via unescaped serialization functions — prompt injection to RCE chain
defense: Never deserialize LLM-influenced data without validation

name: ZombAI (Self-Propagating Coding Agent Worm)
vector: Self-propagating prompt injection worm turns coding agents into malware downloaders and C2 endpoints across repositories
attribution: Johann Rehberger / Embrace The Red (CVE-2025-53773), January-August 2025

name: s1ngularity (AI CLI Weaponization)
vector: Supply chain attack weaponizes AI coding agents as recon tools, coercing automated credential theft via unsafe CLI flags
attribution: Detected by Snyk, GitGuardian, Orca Security; August 2025
targets: AI coding assistants (Claude, Gemini, Amazon Q)
defense: Remove unsafe skip-all-permissions flags from AI tools

name: Flashboom (Code Auditor Blinding)
vector: High-attention code snippets divert LLM code auditor focus away from actual vulnerabilities (96.3% blinding success rate)
attribution: Xiao Li et al., Nanjing University / Drexel University (IEEE S&P 2025)

name: Gemini Calendar Worm (Promptware Propagation)
vector: Calendar invites with hidden prompt injection hijack Gemini agents for tool misuse, cross-agent invocation, and worm-like self-propagation
attribution: SafeBreach / Tel Aviv University, Black Hat USA August 2025
